$comment: Canonical pin for the upstream klzgrad/naiveproxy `naive` binary.
v0.3.0 architecture cut: the SAME upstream binary runs as the
server (in the ct-naive container) AND as the anti-tracking probe
client (bundled in the panel image). Wire-level compatibility is
the whole point of the v0.3.0 split — the v0.2.x drift between
Caddy's `forwardproxy@naive` plugin and the macOS client's bundled
naive binary is exactly what this pin prevents.
Single source of truth. Both Dockerfiles' ARG defaults are kept in
sync with this file by `operator/sync-naive-pin.ts`, which:
- `bun run sync-naive-pin.ts`           rewrites the ARG lines
- `bun run sync-naive-pin.ts --check`   exits non-zero on drift
(wired into `ct update`
preflight and CI).
The bumping workflow:
1. Update this file (upstream_tag + per-arch url/sha256/size).
2. Run `make sync-naive-pin` (rewrites both Dockerfile ARGs).
3. Commit both this file AND the Dockerfile changes together.
4. ALSO bump the macOS client repo's COOL-TUNNEL/naive.upstream
to the same upstream_tag — wire compatibility requires it.
How to refresh on a naive version bump (run from the repo root):
TAG=v149.x.y.z-N
for ARCH in linux-x64 linux-arm64; do
gh api repos/klzgrad/naiveproxy/releases/tags/$TAG \
--jq '.assets[] | select(.name == "naiveproxy-'"$TAG"'-'"$ARCH"'.tar.xz") | {arch:"'$ARCH'", url:.browser_download_url, sha:(.digest|sub("^sha256:";"")), size}'
done
